description:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/landscape/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/landscape/*
or 
$ sudo chgrp landscape /var/log/landscape/*
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/landscape</tt> directory contains information about
the landscape-client and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil_clause:
/var/log/landscape/* does not have a group owner of
root or
landscape
ocil:
To check the group ownership of /var/log/landscape/*, run the command: 
$ ls -lL /var/log/landscape/*
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following group-owner: root or landscape
fixtext:
To properly set the group owner of /var/log/landscape/*, run the command: 
$ sudo chgrp root /var/log/landscape/*
or 
$ sudo chgrp landscape /var/log/landscape/*
title: Verify Groupownership of Files in /var/log/landscape
